channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Diners are staying home, so this restaurant lets patrons pay what they want
item.description: Americans are increasingly passing up on dining out. So one restaurant is allowing diners to pay what they like for their food.
item.content:encoded:
Americans are increasingly passing up on dining out. So one restaurant is allowing diners to pay what they like for their food.

(Image credit: Sergio Flores for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: This big university system is embracing AI. Students and faculty aren't all on board
item.description: The California State University system offers an early look at what happens when an administration commits to a technology that its own community isn't convinced will improve education.
item.content:encoded: The California State University system offers an early look at what happens when an administration commits to a technology that its own community isn't convinced will improve education.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: COMIC: To raise a confident little talker, learn to speak 'parentese'
item.description: So you've got a baby in your life. How do you talk to them, exactly? In this comic, experts explain why conversing with your baby is important — and share ways to help them develop their language skills.
item.content:encoded: So you've got a baby in your life. How do you talk to them, exactly? In this comic, experts explain why conversing with your baby is important — and share ways to help them develop their language skills.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Spin to win: How this life insurance company turns healthy habits into a game
item.description: You've heard of lower car insurance rates for good drivers. Now, one life insurance company wants to incentivize longevity with lower rates and extra bonuses for people who adopt healthy habits.
item.content:encoded:
You've heard of lower car insurance rates for good drivers. Now, one life insurance company wants to incentivize longevity with lower rates and extra bonuses for people who adopt healthy habits.

(Image credit: manusapon kasosod/Moment RF)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: 'My body carried me,' Elizabeth Smart says. Now she's celebrating it
item.description: Her abduction at age 14 drew international attention. After her rescue, Smart says she struggled with feeling shame around her body. Bodybuilding has helped her see herself differently.
item.content:encoded:
Her abduction at age 14 drew international attention. After her rescue, Smart says she struggled with feeling shame around her body. Bodybuilding has helped her see herself differently.

(Image credit: Kim Raff for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump touts breakthrough in negotiations to end Iran war, Middle Eastern countries react to news of a potential deal to end war, Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak.
item.content:encoded: Trump touts breakthrough in negotiations to end Iran war, Middle Eastern countries react to news of a potential deal to end war, Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Muslims begin the annual Hajj against a backdrop of war concerns
item.description: More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.
item.content:encoded:
More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China launches Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 1 of 3 astronauts set for yearlong stay
item.description: China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.
item.content:encoded:
China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: California chemical tank has cracked, causing state of emergency, thousands to evacuate
item.description: One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.
item.content:encoded:
One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.

(Image credit: Apu Gomes)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: DR Congo Ebola cases rise amid distrust, armed conflict zone
item.description: Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.
item.content:encoded:
Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone
